Package org.eventb.core.seqprover

Class ProverFactory

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.ProverFactory
public final class ProverFactory extends Object
Static class with factory methods required to construct various data structures used in the sequent prover. No predicate variables are allowed in any of these methods.
Since:
1.0

  • Method Details

    • reasonerFailure

      public static IReasonerFailure reasonerFailure(IReasoner generatedBy, IReasonerInput generatedUsing, String reason)
      Returns a new reasoner failure object with the given reason.
      Parameters:
      generatedBy - Reasoner used
      generatedUsing - Reasoner Input used
      reason - Reason for reasoner failure
      Returns:
      A reasoner failure object with the given reason.

    • makeProofRule

      public static IProofRule makeProofRule(IReasoner generatedBy, IReasonerInput generatedUsing, Predicate goal, Set<Predicate> neededHyps, Integer confidence, String display, IProofRule.IAntecedent... antecedents)
      Returns a new proof rule with the given information

      This is the most general factory method to construct proof rules. In case the rule to be constructed is more specific, use of a more specific factory method is encouraged.

      Parameters:
      generatedBy - The reasoner used.
      generatedUsing - The reasoner input used.
      goal - The goal of the proof rule, or null iff the proof rule is applicable to a sequent with any goal. In the latter case it is permitted that the antecedents may also contain a null goal.
      neededHyps - The hypotheses needed for the proof rule to be applicable, or null iff no hypotheses are needed.
      confidence - The confidence level of the proof rule, or null iff the greatest confidence level (i.e. IConfidence.DISCHARGED_MAX) is to be used.
      display - The display string for the proof rule, or null iff the reasoner id is to be used.
      antecedents - The antecedents of the proof rule, or null iff this rule has no antecedents.
      Returns:
      A new proof rule with the given information.

    • makeProofRule

      public static IProofRule makeProofRule(IReasonerDesc generatedBy, IReasonerInput generatedUsing, Predicate goal, Set<Predicate> neededHyps, Integer confidence, String display, IProofRule.IAntecedent... antecedents)
      Returns a new proof rule with the given information

      This is similar to makeProofRule(IReasoner, IReasonerInput, Predicate, Set, Integer, String, IAntecedent...) , except that the reasoner is given through its descriptor, which can later be retrieved by calling IReasonerOutput.getReasonerDesc().

      Parameters:
      generatedBy - A descriptor of the reasoner used
      generatedUsing - The reasoner input used
      goal - The goal of the proof rule, or null iff the proof rule is applicable to a sequent with any goal. In the latter case it is permitted that the antecedents may also contain a null goal.
      neededHyps - The hypotheses needed for the proof rule to be applicable, or null iff no hypotheses are needed.
      confidence - The confidence level of the proof rule, or null iff the greatest confidence level (i.e. IConfidence.DISCHARGED_MAX) is to be used.
      display - The display string for the proof rule, or null iff the reasoner id is to be used.
      antecedents - The antecedents of the proof rule, or null iff this rule has no antecedents.
      Returns:
      A new proof rule with the given information.

    • makeProofRule

      public static IProofRule makeProofRule(IReasoner generatedBy, IReasonerInput generatedUsing, Predicate goal, Predicate neededHyp, String display, IProofRule.IAntecedent... antecedents)
      Returns a new proof rule with at most one needed hypothesis and the maximum confidence
      Parameters:
      generatedBy - The reasoner used.
      generatedUsing - The reasoner input used.
      goal - The goal of the proof rule, or null iff the proof rule is applicable to a sequent with any goal.
      neededHyp - The hypothesis needed for the proof rule to be applicable, or null iff no hypotheses are needed.
      display - The display string for the proof rule, or null iff the reasoner id is to be used.
      antecedents - The antecedents of the proof rule, or null iff this rule has no antecedents.
      Returns:
      A new proof rule with the given information.

    • makeProofRule

      public static IProofRule makeProofRule(IReasoner generatedBy, IReasonerInput generatedUsing, Predicate goal, Set<Predicate> neededHyps, String display, IProofRule.IAntecedent... antecedents)
      Returns a new proof rule with a list of needed hypotheses and the maximum confidence
      Parameters:
      generatedBy - The reasoner used
      generatedUsing - The reasoner input used
      goal - The goal of the proof rule, or null iff the proof rule is applicable to a sequent with any goal
      neededHyps - The hypotheses needed for the proof rule to be applicable
      display - The display string for the proof rule, or null iff the reasoner id is to be used
      antecedents - The antecedents of the proof rule, or null iff this rule has no antecedents
      Returns:
      A new proof rule with the given information.
      Since:
      2.0

    • makeProofRule

      public static IProofRule makeProofRule(IReasoner generatedBy, IReasonerInput generatedUsing, Set<Predicate> neededHyps, String display, List<IHypAction> hypActions)
      Returns a new proof rule with needed hypotheses that only contains hypothesis actions and the maximum confidence

      This factory method returns a goal independent rule with one antecedent containing the given hypothesis actions.

      Parameters:
      generatedBy - The reasoner used
      generatedUsing - The reasoner input used
      neededHyps - A set of hypotheses needed by this rule to apply
      display - The display string for the proof rule, or null iff the reasoner id is to be used
      hypActions - The hypothesis actions contained in the rule, or null iff there are no hypothesis actions.
      Returns:
      A new proof rule with the given information.
      Since:
      2.0

    • makeProofRule

      public static IProofRule makeProofRule(IReasoner generatedBy, IReasonerInput generatedUsing, Predicate goal, String display, IProofRule.IAntecedent... antecedents)
      Returns a new proof rule with no needed hypothesis and the maximum confidence
      Parameters:
      generatedBy - The reasoner used.
      generatedUsing - The reasoner input used.
      goal - The goal of the proof rule, or null iff the proof rule is applicable to a sequent with any goal.
      display - The display string for the proof rule, or null iff the reasoner id is to be used.
      antecedents - The antecedents of the proof rule, or null iff this rule has no antecedents.
      Returns:
      A new proof rule with the given information.

    • makeProofRule

      public static IProofRule makeProofRule(IReasoner generatedBy, IReasonerInput generatedUsing, String display, List<IHypAction> hypActions)
      Returns a new proof rule that only contains hypothesis actions.

      This factory method returns a goal independent rule with one antecedent containing the given hypothesis actions.

      Parameters:
      generatedBy - The reasoner used.
      generatedUsing - The reasoner input used.
      display - The display string for the proof rule, or null iff the reasoner id is to be used.
      hypActions - The hypothesis actions contained in the rule, or null iff there are no hypothesis actions.
      Returns:
      A new proof rule with the given information.

    • makeAntecedent

      public static IProofRule.IAntecedent makeAntecedent(Predicate goal, Set<Predicate> addedHyps, FreeIdentifier[] addedFreeIdents, List<IHypAction> hypActions)
      Returns a new antecedent with the given inputs. The constructed antecedent can then be used to construct a proof rule.

      This is a very general factory method to construct antecedents. In case the antecedent to be constructed is more specific, use of a more specific factory method is encouraged.

      Parameters:
      goal - The goal of the antecedent, or null iff the rule is intended to be goal independent.
      addedHyps - The added hypotheses, or null iff there are no added hypotheses.
      addedFreeIdents - The added free identifiers, or null iff there are no added free identifiers.
      hypActions - The hypothesis actions, or null iff there are no hypothesis actions.
      Returns:
      A new antecedent with the given information.

    • makeAntecedent

      public static IProofRule.IAntecedent makeAntecedent(Predicate goal, Set<Predicate> addedHyps, Set<Predicate> unselectedHyps, FreeIdentifier[] addedFreeIdents, List<IHypAction> hypActions)
      Returns a new antecedent with the given inputs. The constructed antecedent can then be used to construct a proof rule.

      This is the most general factory method to construct antecedents. In case the antecedent to be constructed is more specific, use of a more specific factory method is encouraged.

      Parameters:
      goal - The goal of the antecedent, or null iff the rule is intended to be goal independent
      addedHyps - The added hypotheses, or null iff there are no added hypotheses
      unselectedHyps - a subset of added hyps for hypotheses added but not selected, or null if there are no unselected added hypotheses
      addedFreeIdents - The added free identifiers, or null iff there are no added free identifiers
      hypActions - The hypothesis actions, or null iff there are no hypothesis actions
      Returns:
      A new antecedent with the given information
      Since:
      2.0

    • makeAntecedent

      public static IProofRule.IAntecedent makeAntecedent(Predicate goal, Set<Predicate> addedHyps, IHypAction hypAction)
      Returns a new antecedent with no added free identifiers, and at most one hypothesis action.
      Parameters:
      goal - The goal of the antecedent, or null iff the rule is intended to be goal independent.
      addedHyps - The added hypotheses, or null iff there are no added hypotheses.
      hypAction - The hypothesis action, or null iff there are no hypothesis actions.
      Returns:
      A new antecedent with the given information.

    • makeAntecedent

      public static IProofRule.IAntecedent makeAntecedent(Predicate goal)
      Returns a new antecedent that may only specify a goal (i.e. with no added hypotheses, no added free identifiers and no hypothesis actions.)
      Parameters:
      goal - The goal of the antecedent, or null iff the rule is intended to be goal independent.
      Returns:
      A new antecedent with the given information.

    • makeSequent

      public static IProverSequent makeSequent(ITypeEnvironment typeEnv, Collection<Predicate> hyps, Predicate goal)
      Returns a new sequent with the given parameters.

      Note :
      The parameters provided to construct the sequent must be consistent in order to construct a proper sequent. In particular:

      • All predicates (i.e. all hypotheses and the goal) must be type checked.
      • The type environment provided should contain all free identifiers and carrier sets appearing in the predicates of the sequent and can be used to successfully type check them.
      These checks need to be done before calling this method. The behaviour of the sequent prover is undefined if these checks are not done.

      Parameters:
      typeEnv - the type environment for the sequent. This parameter must not be null It should be ensured that all predicates can be type checked using this type environment.
      hyps - The set of hypotheses, or null iff this set is intended to be empty.
      goal - The goal. This parameter must not be null.
      Returns:
      A new sequent with the given parameters.

    • makeSequent

      public static IProverSequent makeSequent(ITypeEnvironment typeEnv, Collection<Predicate> hyps, Collection<Predicate> selHyps, Predicate goal)
      Returns a new sequent with some selected hypotheses from the given parameters.

      Note :
      The parameters provided to construct the sequent must be consistent in order to construct a proper sequent. In particular:

      • All predicates (i.e. all hypotheses and the goal) must be type checked.
      • The type environment provided should contain all free identifiers and carrier sets appearing in the predicates of the sequent and can be used to successfully type check them.
      • All hypotheses to be selected must also be present in the set of hypotheses provided.
      These checks need to be done before calling this method. The behaviour of the sequent prover is undefined if these checks are not done.

      Parameters:
      typeEnv - The type environment for the sequent. This parameter must not be null It should be ensured that all predicates can be type checked using this type environment.
      hyps - The set of hypotheses, or null iff this set is intended to be empty.
      selHyps - The set of hypotheses to select. The set of hypotheses to select should be contained in the set of hypotheses
      goal - The goal. This parameter must not be null.
      Returns:
      A new sequent with the given information.

    • makeSequent

      public static IProverSequent makeSequent(ITypeEnvironment typeEnv, Collection<Predicate> hyps, Collection<Predicate> selHyps, Predicate goal, Object origin)
      Returns a new sequent with some selected hypotheses from the given parameters.

      Note :
      The parameters provided to construct the sequent must be consistent in order to construct a proper sequent. In particular:

      • All predicates (i.e. all hypotheses and the goal) must be type checked.
      • The type environment provided should contain all free identifiers and carrier sets appearing in the predicates of the sequent and can be used to successfully type check them.
      • All hypotheses to be selected must also be present in the set of hypotheses provided.
      These checks need to be done before calling this method. The behaviour of the sequent prover is undefined if these checks are not done.

      Parameters:
      typeEnv - The type environment for the sequent. This parameter must not be null It should be ensured that all predicates can be type checked using this type environment.
      hyps - The set of hypotheses, or null iff this set is intended to be empty.
      selHyps - The set of hypotheses to select. The set of hypotheses to select should be contained in the set of hypotheses
      goal - The goal. This parameter must not be null.
      origin - The origin of the sequent, or null.
      Returns:
      A new sequent with the given information.
      Since:
      2.4

    • makeProofTree

      public static IProofTree makeProofTree(IProverSequent sequent, Object origin)
      Returns a new proof tree with the given sequent at the root.
      Parameters:
      sequent - the sequent of the root node. This parameter must not be null.
      origin - an object describing the origin of the sequent, might be null
      Returns:
      a new proof tree for the given sequent

    • makeSelectHypAction

      public static IHypAction.ISelectionHypAction makeSelectHypAction(Collection<Predicate> toSelect)
      Returns a new select hypotheses action
      Parameters:
      toSelect - Hypotheses to select (should not be null)
      Returns:
      A new select hypotheses action

    • makeDeselectHypAction

      public static IHypAction.ISelectionHypAction makeDeselectHypAction(Collection<Predicate> toDeselect)
      Returns a new deselect hypotheses action
      Parameters:
      toDeselect - Hypotheses to deselect (should not be null)
      Returns:
      A new deselect hypotheses action

    • makeHideHypAction

      public static IHypAction.ISelectionHypAction makeHideHypAction(Collection<Predicate> toHide)
      Returns a new hide hypotheses action
      Parameters:
      toHide - Hypotheses to hide (should not be null)
      Returns:
      A new hide hypotheses action

    • makeShowHypAction

      public static IHypAction.ISelectionHypAction makeShowHypAction(Collection<Predicate> toShow)
      Returns a new show hypotheses action
      Parameters:
      toShow - Hypotheses to show (should not be null)
      Returns:
      A new show hypotheses action

    • makeForwardInfHypAction

      public static IHypAction.IForwardInfHypAction makeForwardInfHypAction(Collection<Predicate> hyps, FreeIdentifier[] addedFreeIdents, Collection<Predicate> inferredHyps)
      Returns a new forward inference hypothesis action

      This is the most general factory method for this construction. In case the construction is more specific, the use of a more specific factory method is encouraged.

      Parameters:
      hyps - The hypotheses required by the forward inference (should not be null)
      addedFreeIdents - Fresh free identifiers added by the forward inference (should not be null)
      inferredHyps - The inferred hypotheses (should not be null)
      Returns:
      A new forward inference hypothesis action

    • makeForwardInfHypAction

      public static IHypAction.IForwardInfHypAction makeForwardInfHypAction(Collection<Predicate> hyps, Collection<Predicate> inferredHyps)
      Returns a new forward inference hypothesis action that does not introduce free identifiers
      Parameters:
      hyps - The hypotheses required by the forward inference (should not be null)
      inferredHyps - The inferred hypotheses (should not be null)
      Returns:
      A new forward inference hypothesis action

    • makeRewriteHypAction

      public static IHypAction.IRewriteHypAction makeRewriteHypAction(Collection<Predicate> neededHyps, FreeIdentifier[] addedFreeIdents, Collection<Predicate> inferredHyps, Collection<Predicate> disappearingHyps)
      Returns a new rewrite hypothesis action (i.e. a forward inference followed by a hide action).

      Disappearing hypotheses are not serialized in the action hypothesis attribute. Thus, disappearing hypotheses shall be added to needed hypotheses when creating an instance of IHypAction.IRewriteHypAction from a serialized proof.

      Parameters:
      neededHyps - the hypotheses required by the forward inference (should not be null)
      addedFreeIdents - fresh free identifiers added by the forward inference (should not be null)
      inferredHyps - the inferred hypotheses (should not be null)
      disappearingHyps - the hypotheses which disappear from the sequent. Disappearing hypotheses are a subset of the needed hypotheses
      Returns:
      a new rewrite hypothesis action which will perform a forward inference followed by a hide action
      Since:
      3.0

    • makeRewriteHypAction

      public static IHypAction.IRewriteHypAction makeRewriteHypAction(Collection<Predicate> neededHyps, Collection<Predicate> inferredHyps, Collection<Predicate> disappearingHyps)
      Returns a new rewrite hypothesis action (i.e. a forward inference followed by a hide action).

      Disappearing hypotheses are not serialized in the action hypothesis attribute. Thus, disappearing hypotheses shall be added to needed hypotheses when creating an instance of IHypAction.IRewriteHypAction from a serialized proof.

      Parameters:
      neededHyps - the hypotheses required by the forward inference (should not be null)
      inferredHyps - the inferred hypotheses (should not be null)
      disappearingHyps - the hypotheses which disappear from the sequent. Disappearing hypotheses are a subset of the needed hypotheses
      Returns:
      a new rewrite hypothesis action which will perform a forward inference followed by a hide action
      Since:
      3.0

    • makeProofDependencies

      public static IProofDependencies makeProofDependencies(boolean hasDeps, Predicate goal, Set<Predicate> usedHypotheses, ISealedTypeEnvironment usedFreeIdents, Set<String> introducedFreeIdents, Set<IReasonerDesc> usedReasoners)
      Constructs an instance of IProofDependencies from the values given as parameters. This is a convenience method. Clients must independently check that the data provided conforms to the constraints in IProofDependencies.
      Parameters:
      hasDeps -
      goal -
      usedHypotheses -
      usedFreeIdents -
      introducedFreeIdents -
      usedReasoners -
      Returns:
      An instance of IProofDependencies with the values given as input parameters
      Since:
      3.0

    • makeSequent

      public static IProverSequent makeSequent(ITypeEnvironment typeEnv, Set<Predicate> globalHypSet, Set<Predicate> hiddenHypSet, Set<Predicate> selectedHypSet, Predicate goal)
      Returns a new sequent with some hidden and selected hypotheses from the given parameters.

      Note :
      The parameters provided to construct the sequent must be consistent in order to construct a proper sequent. In particular:

      • All predicates (i.e. all hypotheses and the goal) must be type checked.
      • The type environment provided should contain all free identifiers and carrier sets appearing in the predicates of the sequent and can be used to successfully type check them.
      • All hypotheses to be hidden must also be present in the set of hypotheses provided.
      • All hypotheses to be selected must also be present in the set of hypotheses provided.
      • All hypotheses to be hidden and selected must be disjoint.
      These checks need to be done before calling this method. The behaviour of the sequent prover is undefined if these checks are not done.

      Parameters:
      typeEnv - The type environment for the sequent. This parameter must not be null. It should be ensured that all predicates can be type checked using this type environment.
      globalHypSet - The set of hypotheses, or null iff this set is intended to be empty.
      hiddenHypSet - The set of hypotheses to hide. The set of hypotheses to hide should be contained in the set of hypotheses
      selectedHypSet - The set of hypotheses to select. The set of hypotheses to select should be contained in the set of hypotheses and disjoint from the set of hidden hypotheses.
      goal - The goal. This parameter must not be null.
      Returns:
      A new sequent with the given information.

    • makeSequent

      public static IProverSequent makeSequent(ITypeEnvironment typeEnv, Set<Predicate> globalHypSet, Set<Predicate> hiddenHypSet, Set<Predicate> selectedHypSet, Predicate goal, Object origin)
      Returns a new sequent with some hidden and selected hypotheses from the given parameters.

      Note :
      The parameters provided to construct the sequent must be consistent in order to construct a proper sequent. In particular:

      • All predicates (i.e. all hypotheses and the goal) must be type checked.
      • The type environment provided should contain all free identifiers and carrier sets appearing in the predicates of the sequent and can be used to successfully type check them.
      • All hypotheses to be hidden must also be present in the set of hypotheses provided.
      • All hypotheses to be selected must also be present in the set of hypotheses provided.
      • All hypotheses to be hidden and selected must be disjoint.
      These checks need to be done before calling this method. The behaviour of the sequent prover is undefined if these checks are not done.

      Parameters:
      typeEnv - The type environment for the sequent. This parameter must not be null. It should be ensured that all predicates can be type checked using this type environment.
      globalHypSet - The set of hypotheses, or null iff this set is intended to be empty.
      hiddenHypSet - The set of hypotheses to hide. The set of hypotheses to hide should be contained in the set of hypotheses
      selectedHypSet - The set of hypotheses to select. The set of hypotheses to select should be contained in the set of hypotheses and disjoint from the set of hidden hypotheses.
      origin - the origin of the sequent
      goal - The goal. This parameter must not be null.
      Returns:
      A new sequent with the given information.
      Since:
      2.4
